verdict.summary: Layer input shape does not match expected shape. Typically a mismatch between Flatten output and Dense input or incorrect transfer learning feature dimensions.

dead_ends.action: Add a Reshape layer to force the shapes to match
dead_ends.why_fails: Reshape does not transform the data meaningfully; it masks the dimensional mismatch and produces garbage output.

dead_ends.action: Remove the failing layer and hope the model still works
dead_ends.why_fails: Removing layers changes the architecture fundamentally. Fix the shape chain instead.

workarounds.action: Use model.summary() to trace shapes through all layers
workarounds.how: model.summary()  # trace input/output shapes layer by layer to find where mismatch occurs

workarounds.action: Set correct input_shape on the first layer matching your data
workarounds.how: model.add(Dense(128, input_shape=(feature_dim,)))  # feature_dim must match X_train.shape[1]

workarounds.action: Use GlobalAveragePooling2D instead of Flatten for transfer learning
workarounds.how: base_model.output -> GlobalAveragePooling2D() -> Dense(num_classes)  # avoids shape dependency on input size
